zh.lifeSummary: 當Rodrigo FERNÁNDEZ DE CASTRO於1090年出生在西班牙時，他的父親Fernando GARCÍA DE HITA, señor de Hita, Uceda, Guadalajara y Medinacelli25歲，母親Trigidia Fernández de Aza31歲。他和Estevainha Peres至少有5個兒子和4個女兒。他去世於1143年10月29日在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安享年53歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Rodrigo FERNÁNDEZ DE CASTRO于1090年出生在西班牙时，他的父亲Fernando GARCÍA DE HITA, señor de Hita, Uceda, Guadalajara y Medinacelli25岁，母亲Trigidia Fernández de Aza31岁。他和Estevainha Peres至少有5个儿子和4个女儿。他去世于1143年10月29日在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安享年53岁。

nameMeanings.name: FERNÁNDEZ
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Juan, Carlos, Luis, Jorge, Miguel, Fernando, Mario, Jesus, Pedro, Francisco.  Portuguese Ligia, Joao, Armanda, Godofredo, Wenceslao.

Spanish (Fernández): patronymic from the personal name Fernando . The surname (and to a lesser extent the variant Hernandez ) has also been established in southern Italy, mainly in Naples and Palermo, since the period of Spanish dominance there.

Jewish (Sephardic): adoption of the name in 1 above at the moment of conversion to Roman Catholicism. After the return to Judaism (generations later), some Jewish descendants retained the surname that their families had adopted as Roman Catholics.

variants.name: Fernando
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Luis, Orlando, Aida, Carlos, Jesusa, Rodolfo, Ruben, Adela, Alfredo, Alicia.  Indian Rohan, Lalith, Nimal, Rajiv, Sanjeeva, Shanthi, Anil, Anusha, Dushan, Kapila, Lakshman, Mallika.

Spanish and Portuguese: from the personal name Fernando, a younger form of Ferdinando (see Ferdinand ). This surname is also found in southern Italy, mainly in Naples and Palermo, since the period of Spanish dominance there, and as a result of the expulsion of the Jews from Spain and Portugal at the end of the 15th century, many of whom moved to Italy. It is also common in western India and especially in Sri Lanka, where it was taken by Portuguese colonists in the 16th century.

nameMeanings.name: DE
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: CASTRO
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Carlos, Luis, Jesus, Jorge, Miguel, Francisco, Mario, Pedro, Raul.  Portuguese Joao, Paulo, Ligia, Vasco, Wenceslao, Armanda, Fernandes.

Galician, Portuguese, and Jewish (Sephardic): topographic name from castro ‘castle, fortress’ (from Latin castrum ‘fort, Roman walled city’), or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word. See also De Castro .

Italian and Jewish (from Italy): topographic name for someone who lived by a fortification, from castro ‘fortification’ (see 1 above) or a habitational name for someone from the town of Castro, the center of a Duchy in Lazio (destroyed after its occupation by Papal military forces in 1649). Compare Lo Castro .

variants.name: Alcazar
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Jose, Francisco, Luis, Manuel, Juan, Julio, Rafael, Raul, Ruben, Alicia, Gustavo.

Spanish (Alcázar): habitational name from any of various places, for example in the provinces of Ciudad Real, Cuenca, and Granada, named with the word alcázar ‘citadel’ or ‘palace’ (from Arabic al ‘the’ + qaṣr ‘fortress’, a borrowing of Latin castrum; compare Castro ).

variants.name: De Castro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Luis, Armando, Mario, Alfredo, Jorge, Julio, Manuel, Ricardo, Rolando, Alvaro.
Galician and Portuguese: habitational name for someone from any of various places called with castro ‘castle, fortress’ (see Castro ), with the preposition de ‘of, from’.

variants.name: Castrillo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Jorge, Angel, Carlos, Emilio, Guillermo, Ramon, Alberto, Alejandro, Ana, Benito.
Spanish: habitational name from any of various places, especially in León, named Castrillo, from castrillo ‘fort’, a diminutive of Castro .

variants.name: Castronovo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Santo, Cosimo, Giacomo, Graziella, Sal, Stefano.
Italian (southern): habitational name from any of various places called Castronovo, from castro ‘fortification’ (see Castro ) + novo ‘new’.

nameMeanings.name: Rodrigo
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Visigothic: composed of the elements hrōd ‘fame’ + rīc ‘power’. It was the name of the last king of the Visigoths, defeated by the Moors in 711 .

en.lifeSummary: When Rodrigo FERNÁNDEZ DE CASTRO was born in 1090, in Spain, his father, Fernando GARCÍA DE HITA, señor de Hita, Uceda, Guadalajara y Medinacelli, was 25 and his mother, Trigidia Fernández de Aza, was 31. He had at least 5 sons and 4 daughters with Estevainha Peres. He died on 29 October 1143, in Castilla y León, Spain, at the age of 53.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Rodrigo FERNÁNDEZ DE CASTRO est né en 1090, à Espagne, son père, Fernando GARCÍA DE HITA, señor de Hita, Uceda, Guadalajara y Medinacelli, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Trigidia Fernández de Aza, avait 31 ans. Il a eu au moins 5 fils et 4 filles avec Estevainha Peres. Il est décédé le 29 octobre 1143, à Castilla y León, Espagne, à l’âge de 53 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Rodrigo FERNÁNDEZ DE CASTRO 1090 in Spanien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Fernando GARCÍA DE HITA, señor de Hita, Uceda, Guadalajara y Medinacelli 25 und seine Mutter Trigidia Fernández de Aza 31. Er hatte mit Estevainha Peres mindestens 5 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb am 29. Oktober 1143 im Alter von 53 Jahren in Castilla y León, Spanien.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Rodrigo FERNÁNDEZ DE CASTRO è nato il nel 1090 nel Spagna, il suo padre, Fernando GARCÍA DE HITA, señor de Hita, Uceda, Guadalajara y Medinacelli, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Trigidia Fernández de Aza, aveva 31 anni. Lui avuto almeno 5 figli e 4 figlie con Estevainha Peres. Morì il 29 ottobre 1143, nel Castilla y León, Spagna, all'età di 53 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Rodrigo FERNÁNDEZ DE CASTROが1090年にスペインで生まれた時，父Fernando GARCÍA DE HITA, señor de Hita, Uceda, Guadalajara y Medinacelliは25歳，母Trigidia Fernández de Azaは31歳であった。彼はEstevainha Peresとの間に少なくとも5人の息子と4人の娘をもうけた。彼は1143年10月29日にスペインカスティラ イ レoンで53歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Rodrigo FERNÁNDEZ DE CASTRO(이)가 1090년 스페인에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Fernando GARCÍA DE HITA, señor de Hita, Uceda, Guadalajara y Medinacelli(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Trigidia Fernández de Aza(은)는 31세였다. 그는 Estevainha Peres(와)과 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1143년 10월 29일 스페인 카스틸라 와이 레o에서 53세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Rodrigo FERNÁNDEZ DE CASTRO nasceu em 1090, em Espanha, seu pai, Fernando GARCÍA DE HITA, señor de Hita, Uceda, Guadalajara y Medinacelli, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Trigidia Fernández de Aza, tinha 31 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 5 filhos e 4 filhas com Estevainha Peres. Ele faleceu em 29 de outubro de 1143, em Castilla y León, Espanha, com 53 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Rodrigo FERNÁNDEZ DE CASTRO родился в 1090 г. в местности Испания, его отцу по имени Fernando GARCÍA DE HITA, señor de Hita, Uceda, Guadalajara y Medinacelli было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Trigidia Fernández de Aza, 31. У него было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери от Estevainha Peres. Он умер 29 октября 1143 г. в местности кастилла и леoн, Испания в возрасте 53 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Rodrigo FERNÁNDEZ DE CASTRO nació en 1090, en España, su papá, Fernando GARCÍA DE HITA, señor de Hita, Uceda, Guadalajara y Medinacelli, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Trigidia Fernández de Aza, tenía 31 años. Tuvo por lo menos 5 hijos y 4 hijas con Estevainha Peres. Murió el 29 de octubre de 1143, en Castilla y León, España, a la edad de 53 años.
